title: The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb}} {{Otheruses|Sunberry}} {{LiveShow |image=[[File:Ewoks_on_Ice_poster.jpg]] |name="The Ewoks
and the Magic Sunberries" |director=[[Robert Turk]]''Bravo America: Bold-Bright-Beautiful'' program |writer=[[Anne Snyder]] |music= |basis=[[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] |premiere date=[[January]] [[1986]]''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]'' |closing date= |premiere location= |productions=1986 [[Wikipedia:Ice Capades|Ice Capades]] |timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] multimedia series, which include the events of "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries," to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |series=''Ewoks'' }} {{Quote|What's new in Ice Capades? Five dramatic productions, dazzling special effects, innovative choreography, and exciting live music. The Ewoks add their touch of magic. It's a surprise you won't want to miss.|Ice Capades commercial excerpt|audio=IceCapades-Commercial.flac|{{YouTube|video=o-21zfzO4Lc|text=Ice Capades December 1986 TV commercial|channel=channel/UCioQmyB0WnsT9jwMczLpI7g|name=robatsea2009}} }} "'''The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries'''," also referred to as "'''Ewoks on Ice'''," was a [[1986]] American ice show based on the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']]. It was a segment of the traveling [[Wikipedia:Ice Capades|Ice Capades]] variety show ''Bravo America: Bold-Bright-Beautiful'', which was produced and directed by [[Wikipedia:United States Figure Skating Hall of Fame|Figure Skating Hall of Fame]] inductee [[Robert Turk]]. The [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] skit was written by [[Anne Snyder]], and it was split into two acts that spanned ''Bravo America''{{'s}} intermission. Billed for ages "3 to 93," the Ewoks segment featured musical numbers starring characters from the ''Ewoks'' cartoon, including [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]], and the antagonistic [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]]. "Ice Capades and ''Ewoks''" souvenirs were available for purchase at the shows, and some items have become rare collectibles. In [[2005]], [[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]] ranked "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" as the second-goofiest moment of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Expanded Universe]]. ==Plot summary== {{Listen3|EwokRap.flac|A poem from "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries"|The poem was featured in the show, and its text was included in the show's program.}} On the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] residents of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] [[Music/Legends|sing]] and dance around [[Fire/Legends|bonfires]] each [[Standard day/Legends|night]]. One night, the [[Bright Tree Village tribe|tribe's]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], tells a story about life beyond the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] moon. However, the Ewoks' [[Ewok–Dulok wars|warring]] cousins, the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]], set out to cause trouble. An adventure ensues, and Ewoks including [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]], [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], Logray, [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], and [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]] are pitted against the Dulok [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]], [[Urgah]], and several Dulok [[Scout/Legends|scouts]]. In the excitement, the two tribes discover [[Magic/Legends|magic]] [[Sunberry/Legends|sunberries]]. ==Development== {{Quote|A Tale For Kids, 3 To 93!|Program tagline|"[[The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries]]"}} [[File:IceCapades-Tulsa.jpg|thumb|right|250px|"The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries," photographed at the 1986 Tulsa State Fair]] "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries," also referred to as "Ewoks on Ice,"{{Blog|abelgpena|35|Ewoks at the Ice Capades}} was an ice show featured in the [[1986]] [[Wikipedia:Ice Capades|Ice Capades]] variety show ''Bravo America: Bold-Bright-Beautiful''. It was divided into two scenes, "The Ewok Village Square" and "Later That Night," which bridged ''Bravo America''{{'s}} intermission. The Ewoks production was preceded and followed by the acts "Power Plus!" and "Romeo and Juliet," respectively. ''Bravo America'' was directed and produced by Ice Capades veteran [[Robert Turk]], and the Ewoks segment was written by [[Anne Snyder]] and based on the [[1985]] animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} Costumes for "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" were designed and produced by [[Joseph Porro]].{{WebCite|url=https://www.pinterest.com/pin/421508846352628941|text=Ice Capade|author=Joseph Porro|work=[https://www.pinterest.com/jporro1 Joseph Porro Costume Style]|quote=I was in charge of the Ewok costume production and made most of these walk around heads here.|archivedate=20200130234157}} The ''Bravo America'' tour was performed by professional ice skaters in rinks across the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]].{{InsiderCite|83|20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe}} These performances included appearances at the [[Wikipedia:Nassau Coliseum|Nassau Coliseum]] and [[Wikipedia:Meadowlands Arena|Meadowlands Arena]] in [[January]] 1986; [[Wikipedia:Madison Square Garden|Madison Square Garden]] and the [[Wikipedia:Capital Centre|Capital Centre]] in [[February]] 1986;{{YouTube|video=M6mYyi_S_9Y|text=Ice Capades featuring the Ewoks Commercial (1986)|channel=channel/UC3uQLazuPUE8JPQq3dg9ZvA|name=Dino Drac}}{{WebCite|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/lifestyle/1986/02/13/ice-capades-capital-show/cdab2e29-efa5-420e-aa7b-122e2ddc31a6|text=Ice Capades: Capital Show!|author=Stokes, W. Royal|date=1986-02-13|work=[https://www.washingtonpost.com The Washington Post]|quote=All of this was topped by a troupe of Ewoks and their archenemies the Duloks in a charming skit that included musical instruction in that arcane linguistic phenomenon, Ewokese.|archivedate=20200204042819}} [[Wikipedia:Chicago Stadium|Chicago Stadium]] in [[March]] 1986;{{WebCite|url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/news/ct-xpm-1986-03-19-8601200677-story.html|text=ICE CAPADES CAPTURES CROWD WITH CLASS, DAZZLE|author=Blau, Robert|date=1986-03-19|work=[https://www.chicagotribune.com Chicago Tribune]|quote=And when the larger-than-life Ewoks enter the ice, the children are further entranced by the ''Star Wars'' characters' search for the mystical sunberries. Will the Ewoks recover the magical fruit? In a clever ploy, the answer must wait until intermission is over.|archivedate=20200203220434}} and the [[Wikipedia:Tulsa State Fair|Tulsa State Fair]] in [[September]] 1986.{{DeviantArt|username=wallbie|url=wallbie/art/Timewarp-Tues-A-Star-Wars-Day-Late-531136719|name=Brandon|text=Timewarp Tues. A Star Wars Day Late}} Ice Capades also toured in [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canada]], performing at the [[Wikipedia:Pacific Coliseum|Pacific Coliseum]] in January [[1987]]. [[File:Ewoks_on_Ice_program.jpg|thumb|left|275px|''Bravo America''{{'s}} program featured a two-page spread for "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries."]] The show's program contained three pages for the Ewoks production—a one sheet and a two-page spread—and billed it as "A Tale For Kids, 3 To 93!" The spread featured a poem from the show and images captioned "Princess Kneesaa, Logray, Chief Chirpa, Wicket" and "Wicket And The Duloks." Some programs also included an image labeled "Princess Kneesaa and friends." A line of "Ice Capades and ''Ewoks''" merchandise that included pennants, buttons, and fiber optic flashlights were available for purchase at the Ice Capades shows. Additionally, [[Wikipedia:KFC|KFC]] sponsored an ''Ewoks'' coloring contest and gave out tickets, and [[Wikipedia:McDonald's|McDonald's]] offered admission coupons.{{YouTube|video=fRUv-qPQXGY|text=Star Wars Celebration Anaheim Collecting Track Panel: Ewok Collectibles|channel=channel/UCQx0LrI5XV1yBfaUi4mlS_w|name=Tommy Garvey}} The show was further promoted by televised commercials. One such commercial aired during "[[The Season Scepter]]," the thirteenth episode of ''Ewoks''{{'}} [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]]. ==Release and reception== {{Quote|And you thought the ''Star Wars Holiday Special'' was Lucasfilm's dirtiest secret.|Abel G. Peña|}} [[File:Ewoks_on_Ice_Urgah.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The costume for the Dulok Urgah, designed by Joseph Porro]] In [[2005]], the [[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]] article "[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]," written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Enrique Guerrero]], ranked "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" as the second-goofiest moment of the Expanded Universe; the third-ranked moment was ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'', and the first was "[[Planet of Kadril]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic strip]] in which [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] wore drag-like clothing while opposing the [[Pacifog]], a weapon which brought out the worst traits of individuals. In an accompanying [[Star Wars: Blogs|blog]] post, Peña referred to "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" as "[[Lucasfilm]]'s dirtiest secret." ==Continuity== "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" was based on the television series ''Ewoks'' and set in the year 3 ABY. In the ice show, the Ewok Wicket costume corresponded to ''Ewoks''{{'}} first season, with the character wearing an [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Clothing/Legends|hood]];{{Ewoks|The Cries of the Trees}} in ''Ewoks''{{'}} second season, Wicket's hood was green.{{Ewoks|The Crystal Cloak}} "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" was a part of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, and it is not included in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]].{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} ==Legacy== Robert Turk, who had directed Ice Capades shows since [[1960s|1965]], retired after the 1986 show and later wrote that "those were the happiest years of my entire career." In [[2010]], he was inducted into the [[Wikipedia:United States Figure Skating Hall of Fame|United States Figure Skating Hall of Fame]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.worldskatingmuseum.org/documents/BobTurkobit.pdf|text=Robert "Bob" Turk, 1925-2017|date=2017|work=[https://www.worldskatingmuseum.org World Figure Skating Museum and Hall of Fame]|archivedate=20200131161557}} Ice Capades costumer Joseph Porro later returned to ''Star Wars'', becoming the costume designer for the [[2019]] [[Disney+]] series ''[[Star Wars: The Mandalorian]]''.{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 1: The Mandalorian}} "The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries" was a topic of discussion for collector's panels at [[Celebration IV]] in [[2007]]{{SWArchive|url=community/event/celebration/news20070221.html|text=Collector Panels and More at Celebration IV}} and [[Celebration Anaheim (2015)|Celebration Anaheim]] in 2015. According to the 2010 book ''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]'', the show's pennants have become rare collectibles. ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= |crew= *Director: [[Robert Turk]] *Producer: Robert Turk *Writer: [[Anne Snyder]] *Costume designer: [[Joseph Porro]] }} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|soundcat=Audio files from The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries|imagecat=Images from The Ewoks and the Magic Sunberries}} {{App |characters= *[[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] *[[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]] *[[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]]
